title.en: NDA (Non-Disclosure Agreement) — What to Include, What's Enforceable in India

answerBox.en: An NDA protects your business information before you share it — with vendors, hires, investors or collaborators. The essentials: a precise definition of confidential information, permitted use, term (2–5 years typical), exclusions, and remedies including injunctions. Confidentiality clauses are enforceable in India; blanket non-competes after termination generally are not (Section 27, Contract Act).

en.title: Define "confidential information" precisely
en.summary: Too broad = unenforceable; too narrow = unprotected.
en.detail: List what is actually being protected: **customer lists, pricing, source code, formulas, business plans, financials** — and mark whether oral disclosures count (require written confirmation within days for provability). Include standard **exclusions**: information already public, independently developed, or lawfully received from others. Courts enforce specific, reasonable definitions; "everything ever discussed" invites the agreement being read down.

en.title: Choose mutual or one-way, and the permitted use
en.summary: The information may be used ONLY for the stated purpose.
en.detail: A **one-way NDA** fits vendors and hires receiving your information; a **mutual NDA** fits partnerships and M&A talks. The heart is the **purpose clause**: information may be used solely for evaluating/performing the stated engagement — using your client list to poach clients breaches even without "disclosure". Add non-solicitation of clients/staff during the term (reasonable restraints operating during a contract are enforceable).

en.title: Know the Indian enforceability lines
en.summary: Confidentiality: yes. Post-term non-compete: mostly no.
en.detail: Indian courts enforce confidentiality and trade-secret protection through damages and — critically — **injunctions** restraining use/disclosure. But **Section 27, Contract Act** voids agreements restraining lawful trade: a blanket "shall not work in this industry for 2 years after leaving" is generally unenforceable against employees, while confidentiality itself survives termination. Draft to protect information, not to imprison people — that distinction is what survives court.

en.title: Remedies and execution mechanics
en.summary: Injunction clause, damages, arbitration, and proper signing.
en.detail: Provide expressly for **injunctive relief** (acknowledging damages are inadequate), damages/account of profits, **arbitration** with interim-relief carve-out (Section 9 Arbitration Act lets you rush to court for injunctions), governing law and jurisdiction. Execute on ₹100–500 stamp paper (state-wise) with signatures of authorised signatories. On breach: preserve evidence, send the cease-and-desist through an advocate, and move for interim injunction fast — delay is the enemy of secrecy remedies.

rows.label: Free basic template
rows.note: Our NDA template for simple cases (below)

en.footnote: An NDA is deterrence plus a litigation foundation — pair it with practical controls (access limits, watermarks, need-to-know sharing), because the best trade-secret case is the leak that never happened.

en.q: Are NDAs actually enforceable in India?
en.a: Yes — confidentiality obligations are ordinary contract terms, enforced through injunctions and damages; Indian courts routinely restrain ex-employees and counterparties from using trade secrets. What fails is the overreach: post-termination non-competes and definitions so broad they restrain trade rather than protect information.

en.q: How long should an NDA last?
en.a: Two to five years for general business information; genuine trade secrets (formulas, source code) deserve a carve-out lasting as long as the information stays secret. Perpetual terms for everything look unreasonable and invite reading-down — tier the durations by information type.

en.q: Someone breached my NDA. What do I actually do?
en.a: Move in this order, fast: preserve evidence of the breach (emails, the leaked material, witnesses), advocate's cease-and-desist notice, then interim injunction — via Section 9 Arbitration Act if you have an arbitration clause, else civil court — plus damages/account of profits. Speed matters; months of inaction undermines the "irreparable harm" injunctions require.

en.q: Do investors sign NDAs before a pitch?
en.a: VCs almost never do — they see too many similar decks and refuse liability. Protect yourself instead by staging disclosure: pitch the what, hold back the how (code, formulas, unit economics detail) until term-sheet stage, and watermark materials. NDAs are for vendors, hires, manufacturers and acquirers — not first pitches.

Yes — electronic execution is recognised under the IT Act, and courts accept e-signed agreements; keep the signing trail (emails, e-sign certificates). Stamp duty compliance still applies for use in evidence, and can be regularised by adjudication if missed.

reviewer: Adv. Sneha Iyer, Bar Council of Tamil Nadu & Puducherry, TN/1187/2013
